bun.context: Elevated in renal failure, dehydration, GI bleed

creatinine.context: Elevated in renal insufficiency. Use CKD-EPI or Cockcroft-Gault for GFR

calcium.context: Correct for albumin: add 0.8 per 1.0 below albumin 4.0

magnesium.context: Low Mg can cause refractory hypokalemia

albumin.context: Low in liver disease, nephrotic syndrome, malnutrition

hemoglobin.context: Female 12-16, Male 14-17.5. Anemia if below range

wbc.context: Leukocytosis >11, Leukopenia <4.5. Left shift if bands >10%

pt.context: Prolonged in warfarin use, liver disease, DIC

ptt.context: Prolonged in heparin use, hemophilia, lupus anticoagulant

fibrinogen.context: Low in DIC, liver failure. Acute phase reactant (rises in inflammation)

d-dimer.context: Elevated in PE, DVT, DIC, sepsis. High sensitivity, low specificity

troponin.context: Elevated in MI, myocarditis, PE, renal failure. High-sensitivity <14 ng/L

bnp.context: Heart failure: >400 likely, 100-400 gray zone. Age-adjusted: >age*50 if >75

ast.context: Elevated in liver damage, MI, hemolysis. AST>ALT suggests alcoholic liver

alt.context: More specific for liver than AST. ALT>AST suggests viral/NASH

alp.context: Elevated in cholestasis, bone disease, pregnancy

bilirubin.context: Total. Direct >0.3 suggests conjugated/obstructive. Indirect: hemolysis, Gilbert

ggt.context: Elevated in cholestasis, alcohol use. Helps distinguish bone vs liver ALP

ldh.context: Elevated in hemolysis, tissue damage, lymphoma, PCP pneumonia

ferritin.context: Female 12-150, Male 12-300. Iron deficiency <12. Very high in HLH, Still disease

iron.context: Low in iron deficiency, chronic disease. High in hemochromatosis

tibc.context: High TIBC + low iron = iron deficiency. Low TIBC = chronic disease

crp.context: Acute phase reactant. >10 suggests bacterial infection

esr.context: Female 0-20, Male 0-15. Elevated in inflammation, infection, malignancy

procalcitonin.context: >0.5 suggests bacterial infection. >2.0 high risk sepsis

free t4.context: Low in hypothyroid, High in hyperthyroid

lactate.context: Elevated in sepsis, shock, ischemia. >4 = severe

ammonia.context: Elevated in hepatic encephalopathy, urea cycle defects

lipase.context: Elevated in pancreatitis (>3x upper limit significant)

amylase.context: Less specific than lipase for pancreatitis

uric acid.context: Gout risk >7.0. Tumor lysis syndrome causes acute elevation

anion gap.context: Elevated: MUDPILES (Methanol, Uremia, DKA, Propylene glycol, INH/Iron, Lactic acidosis, Ethylene glycol, Salicylates)
